title: OCR Error Detection Hotfix
body:
Fix input handling of error detection model: 
- Segment length is automatically handled by tokenizer, and is by `#tokens`, not `#chars`, removing the splitting logic inside `marker`
- Join lines using `\n`

step_plan.summary: Remove Marker-side character-based splitting for OCR error detection input and ensure the model input is built from lines joined with newline characters, delegating length handling to the tokenizer/model (token-based).

steps.operation: Adjust model wiring/config to rely on tokenizer-managed max token length (and remove/avoid any character-based segmentation assumptions) for the OCR error detection predictor
steps.reason: The PR’s hotfix requires that segment length be handled by the tokenizer/model in tokens rather than by Marker in characters. The model construction point is the correct place to ensure the OCRErrorPredictor is instantiated/configured without any Marker-side chunking expectations (e.g., no max-char parameters propagated, and any max_length/truncation behavior delegated to the tokenizer).
steps.side_effects: May change runtime behavior on long documents (fewer per-chunk calls, potentially different truncation behavior). Downstream code that expected per-chunk outputs may need alignment updates. Should re-run any inference/regression tests around OCR error detection; if no tests exist, add a minimal sanity test to ensure long inputs don’t crash and that newline-joined text is accepted.
